rag_rules: Place input assumptions and output guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are available
Use only visible identifiers from the current component type or its ports in AGREE contracts
Write guarantees that relate outputs to inputs, avoiding subcomponent paths in type annexes
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts in component types
generic_rules: Use concrete bounds or named constants in range expressions
Avoid implementation-specific details in component type contracts
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `guarantee` for guarantees
Comparison expressions are bool; integer literals and arithmetic are int; real literals and arithmetic are real
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not place assumptions or guarantees in implementation annexes unless they are implementation-specific
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
